shots.video_prompt: The Archive keeper host speaks directly to camera in English, lip-synced, measured documentary gravitas, mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Delivers slowly: "Before Latin. Before Greek. Before anything was written down — there was one language. No one ever recorded it. And yet we can rebuild it… from its children." Subtle slow push-in, warm brass lamp on face, hands near open codex on worktable.
shots.speech_text: Before Latin. Before Greek. Before anything was written down — there was one language. No one ever recorded it. And yet we can rebuild it… from its children.

shots.video_prompt: Host push-in medium-close, speaks signature beat in English, lip-synced, thoughtful pause between clauses, mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Delivers: "What did they actually say? … And how do we prove it?" Still grounded, trustworthy delivery.
shots.speech_text: What did they actually say? … And how do we prove it?

shots.video_prompt: The same Archive host now speaks reconstructed Proto-Indo-European slowly and clearly, lip-synced, scholarly documentary tone, never claiming it is a recording. PIE line: "h₂éwis, h₁éḱwōs h₁éḱontes" IPA target [ˈh₂é.wis ˈh₁é.kʷoːs ˈh₁é.kʷon.tes]. shots.on_screen:
*h₂éwis, h₁éḱwōs h₁éḱontes
[ˈh₂é.wis ˈh₁é.kʷoːs ˈh₁é.kʷon.tes]

shots.video_prompt: Host back in Archive, gestures toward manuscripts on table while explaining in English, lip-synced, mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Delivers: "We compare its daughters — Latin, Sanskrit, Old English — and work backward to the sound that must have stood behind them all. That's the comparative method. It's reconstruction, not a recording — and we'll always tell you which is which." Educational hand gesture toward codex, calm authority, honesty about uncertainty.
shots.speech_text: We compare its daughters — Latin, Sanskrit, Old English — and work backward to the sound that must have stood behind them all. That's the comparative method. It's reconstruction, not a recording — and we'll always tell you which is which.
shots.on_screen: Comparative method · Latin ovis ← *h₂éwis · Sanskrit ávi- · English ewe

shots.video_prompt: Host warm close, speaks tagline in English, lip-synced, mid-low resonant unhurried voice, precise diction, documentary gravitas, Attenborough-calm, accessible never stuffy, synthetic host only. Delivers: "Bring the dead tongues back — one attestation at a time. DAVID." Slight smile of quiet wonder, medium close-up.
shots.speech_text: Bring the dead tongues back — one attestation at a time. DAVID.
